summary: postcss-minify-selector-parser typosquats postcss-selector-parser and executes an AES-obfuscated payload via new Function
details: Package name is a 2-word insertion typosquat against the widely-used postcss-selector-parser. src/index.js re-exports the real postcss-selector-parser as `selectorParser` to provide a working facade, while src/config/defaults.js ships a multi-kilobyte AES-GCM ciphertext (DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT) together with its decryption material (DEFAULT_AES_PASSPHRASE='default-dev-passphrase', DEFAULT_AES_SALT='encode-npm-c-salt'). src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js:51 decrypts the blob and invokes `new Function('require', runnable)(require)` on the plaintext, executing arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript with full Node privileges (including `require`, so any module — child_process, fs, http, net — is reachable). Shipping the passphrase alongside the ciphertext means the AES layer provides no confidentiality; it is obfuscation to evade scanners. Multiple entry points trigger the decrypt-and-exec path: package.json declares `start`, `decode`, `example`, and `test:cjs` scripts, and runtime/lib.min.js, cjs-runner.js, scripts/cjs-runner.js, and examples/run-custom-flow.js are thin wrappers around `require('../src').run()`. A developer who installs this expecting postcss-selector-parser, or who invokes `npm start` / any of the shipped runner scripts, executes the embedded payload. No legitimate selector-parser library needs an encrypted runnable blob.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
